problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Glial cell activation/suppression in target brain region
hypothesis: Glial cell activation/suppression in target brain region may resolve Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? by activating at the systems level.

experiment.protocol: EEG/MEG signal analysis with 25 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: To know that you do not know is the best. — Lao Tzu
